title: 'YouTube for all the senses' — Dec 2018 product-concept seed
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Dec 27, 2018 (Yahir age ~16) — "YouTube for all the senses - Concept." Two days after Christmas. No body. The format <established-product> for <new-axis> is the classic teen-founder pitch shape, and this is the earliest specimen in the corpus.
  • The idea: extend video to taste/smell/touch/proprioception — a sensorimotor-internet thesis that anticipates VR/haptics startups by ~5 years and is structurally close to what Apple Vision Pro / Meta Quest would later build (without the smell/taste rails). At 16, in a small-town Apple Note, with no exposure to the relevant labs.
  • Sits inside the December 2018 mini-cluster of seed-thoughts: Dec 1 manifesto, Dec 24 Pokémon type self portrait, and this Dec 27 product-concept. Three notes spanning identity / philosophy / venture in 26 days.
  • Earliest specimen of the disruption-shaped thinking that gets explicit in the August 2019 2019 08 12 keep things simple "how to disrupt" column. The 2018 Yahir wasn't yet writing the disrupt-frame in words — he was already doing it in product nouns.
  • Also a FITFO-precursor in concept-naming: capture the idea fast, don't elaborate, trust future-self to develop. Same instinct that produced the water analogy note four months earlier.

Key quote

"YouTube for all the senses - Concept"

Why it matters in the graph

  • The earliest "build this" product flash in the corpus, beating the 2019 e-commerce / subscription-box ideas in 2019 08 18 contact yebw people by 8 months.
  • "For all the senses" is the axis-of-extension move — the same shape as later AI visibility (extend SEO to LLM-result space) and BroMenu (extend menus to nutrition-ranking space). At 16 he's already doing the what existing-thing-plus-new-dimension pattern that drives every venture in this vault.
  • Makes the "college dreams lady whose brother works in AI" reference in 2019 11 13 coding zeal less surprising — Yahir's been frontier-curious since at least Christmas 2018.

Open questions

  • Was this triggered by a Black Mirror episode, a Wired piece, or pure invention?
  • Did the multi-sense thread ever get developed, or stay at title-stage?
  • Worth a densify check: is there an FBLA pitch or business-class essay from 2019-2020 that elaborates this idea?

Wikilinks
